text.title: Embedding Generation Rules
text.summary: Concrete rules for model selection, batching, caching, normalization, and common pitfalls in embedding generation pipelines.

rule.statement: Use the same embedding model for indexing and querying; never mix models within a single collection.
rule.rationale: Each model defines its own vector space. Querying with a different model than was used for indexing produces incorrect similarity scores because the vectors live in incompatible spaces.

rule.statement: Pin the model name and provider in a config file before indexing begins; treat any model change as a full re-index event.
rule.rationale: A model change invalidates every stored vector. Config-pinning makes the dependency explicit and prevents accidental drift.

rule.statement: For OpenAI text-embedding-3 models, prefer native dimension reduction over post-hoc PCA when storage is constrained.
rule.rationale: Native reduction (via the `dimensions` parameter) preserves the model's internal geometry; PCA applied after the fact discards directions the model considers important.

rule.statement: Always batch embedding requests; never call the API once per text in a loop.
rule.rationale: API round-trip latency dominates at small batch sizes. Batching cuts wall-clock time by 10-100x and reduces rate-limit exposure.

rule.statement: Cap OpenAI embedding batches at 2048 items; validate before sending to avoid a 400 error.
rule.rationale: The API enforces a hard 2048-item-per-request limit. Exceeding it raises a non-retryable error.

rule.statement: Sort OpenAI batch response by `item.index` before mapping back to inputs.
rule.rationale: The API may return embeddings out of order. Skipping the sort step produces misaligned embeddings for shuffled input lists.

rule.statement: Cache embeddings keyed by hash(model + text); retrieve from cache before calling the API.
rule.rationale: Repeated texts (common in RAG pipelines that re-ingest updated documents) waste API budget without caching.

rule.statement: Use SHA-256 for cache keys in production; MD5 has non-zero collision probability at billion-document scale.
rule.rationale: MD5 collisions exist in practice. A collision silently returns the wrong embedding for a text.

rule.statement: Normalize embeddings to unit length before storing when cosine similarity is the intended metric.
rule.rationale: Pre-normalization reduces query-time computation to a dot product, which is faster than the full cosine formula and numerically identical for unit vectors.

rule.statement: Guard against zero-length vectors before normalizing; skip or flag them rather than dividing by zero.
rule.rationale: Empty strings and whitespace-only texts can produce near-zero vectors that cause division-by-zero or NaN propagation.

antipattern.description: Embedding texts that exceed the model's token limit without truncation.
antipattern.reason: Some SDKs silently truncate overlong inputs; others raise an error. Either way, the tail of the text is lost, producing embeddings that do not represent the full content.

antipattern.description: Instantiating multiple SentenceTransformer model instances in the same process.
antipattern.reason: Each instance loads the full model into GPU/CPU RAM. Two instances of bge-large-en-v1.5 on a 16 GB GPU will OOM.

antipattern.description: Using Euclidean distance on unnormalized vectors when cosine similarity was intended.
antipattern.reason: Euclidean distance conflates vector magnitude with direction. Cosine similarity requires normalization to measure directional agreement alone.

antipattern.description: Calling the Ollama embedding endpoint in a per-text loop without batching.
antipattern.reason: Ollama has no native batch endpoint; the loop is O(n) HTTP calls. For large corpora this is impractically slow.
